meta.description: Cross-reference of DFR platforms against grant programs. The authoritative source for 'can I buy this platform with that grant.' Updated quarterly and when grant rules change.
meta.disclaimer: This matrix is for informational purposes. Always verify current eligibility with your State Administrative Agency (SAA) and grant program office before purchase. Rules change.
sources: Ohio ODOT DFR Pilot Program documentation

grant_programs.name: COPS Technology and Equipment Program
grant_programs.notes: NDAA §848 compliance mandatory. Blue UAS preferred but not required — non-Blue platforms need manufacturer NDAA cert letter.
grant_programs.amount_fy2025: Invitational program — award size varies by agency size and project scope; typically $50K–$500K per agency
grant_programs.amount_note: FY2025 TEP is invitational only — agencies must be invited by COPS Office. Not open application. FY2026 NOFO not yet released.
grant_programs.application_cycle: Invitational — COPS Office selects agencies; check cops.usdoj.gov for NOFO announcements
grant_programs.fy2026_status: FY2026 NOFO not yet released (as of April 2026)
grant_programs.drone_act_note: DRONE Act pending in Congress would explicitly authorize Byrne-JAG and COPS grants for drone purchase/operation

grant_programs.name: Homeland Security Grant Program — SHSP
grant_programs.notes: Must use AEL item 03OE-07-SUAS. NDAA and ASDA compliance both required. Apply through SAA — not directly to FEMA.
grant_programs.amount_fy2025: $373.5 million total SHSP nationally (FY2025). State allocations vary by risk formula.
grant_programs.amount_note: FY2025 total HSGP = $1.008 billion across SHSP + UASI + OPSG. FY2026 NOFO not yet released nationally.
grant_programs.typical_award_range: Varies by state allocation — individual subrecipient awards typically $25K–$2M
grant_programs.application_cycle: Annual. Apply through State Administrative Agency (SAA) — NOT directly to FEMA. State deadlines vary (example: Iowa NOI Feb 27, application Mar 27, 2026).
grant_programs.fy2026_status: FY2026 federal NOFO not yet released; state sub-grant applications open in some states (Q1 2026)
grant_programs.passthrough_requirement: States must pass at least 80% of HSGP funds to local/tribal governments within 45 days
grant_programs.letpa_requirement: 35% of award must support law enforcement terrorism prevention activities (FY2025)

grant_programs.name: Homeland Security Grant Program — UASI
grant_programs.notes: Higher award amounts than SHSP. Requires THIRA alignment. Same NDAA/ASDA/AEL requirements as SHSP.
grant_programs.amount_fy2025: $553.5 million total UASI nationally (FY2025). 31 designated urban areas only.
grant_programs.amount_note: Higher per-area amounts than SHSP. FY2026 NOFO not yet released.
grant_programs.typical_award_range: Varies by urban area risk score — major metros (NYC, LA, Chicago) receive $50M+; smaller designated areas may receive $5–15M
grant_programs.application_cycle: Annual through SAA. Eligibility limited to 100 most populous MSAs by risk assessment.
grant_programs.fy2026_status: FY2026 federal NOFO not yet released
grant_programs.apply_via: Designated urban area homeland security office through state SAA
grant_programs.thira_requirement: Must align with Threat and Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (THIRA)

grant_programs.name: Ohio DFR Pilot Program
grant_programs.notes: Must use ODOT pre-approved vendor list (closed Jan 1 2026). Approved vendors include Skydio, BRINC, Parrot. Reimbursement-based.
grant_programs.amount_fy2025: Program closed — vendor list closed January 1, 2026. Prior awards typically $100K–$500K per municipality.
grant_programs.amount_note: Ohio DFR Pilot Program vendor list is CLOSED as of Jan 1 2026. New applications not accepted. Existing awardees continue.
grant_programs.fy2026_status: Program closed to new applicants as of January 1, 2026
grant_programs.reimbursement_model: Cost reimbursement — agencies purchase then submit receipts

grant_programs.name: FEMA BRIC / Hazard Mitigation
grant_programs.notes: Less common DFR pathway. Relevant for SAR and disaster response programs. Verify current eligibility with FEMA.
grant_programs.amount_fy2025: $1.0 billion total BRIC nationally (FY2025). Competitive — not guaranteed.
grant_programs.amount_note: BRIC is highly competitive. Drone/SAR projects compete with all hazard mitigation projects. Success rate varies.
grant_programs.typical_award_range: $100K–$5M for technology projects; larger for infrastructure
grant_programs.application_cycle: Annual. FY2025 application closed. FY2026 expected Q3 2026.
grant_programs.fy2026_status: FY2026 NOFO expected Q3 2026 — watch grants.gov
grant_programs.apply_via: Through state hazard mitigation officer (SHMO) — not directly to FEMA
grant_programs.use_case_fit: Best fit for SAR and disaster response DFR programs; less suited for routine law enforcement DFR

grant_programs.name: Counter-Unmanned Aircraft Systems (C-UAS) Grant Program
grant_programs.typical_award_range: Based on risk score — 11 FIFA host states prioritized in FY2026
grant_programs.application_cycle: FY2026: Application Oct 28–Dec 5, 2025. Awards announced Dec 30, 2025. FY2027 open to all 56 SAAs.
grant_programs.fy2026_status: FY2026 awards announced December 30, 2025. FY2027 round coming.
grant_programs.purpose: Detect, identify, track, or monitor UAS threats. Supports C-UAS tech acquisition for public safety.
grant_programs.notes: NEW program — most significant new DFR-adjacent funding since HSGP. FY2027 opens to all states. Established by One Big Beautiful Bill Act 2025.
grant_programs.warning: FY2026 limited to FIFA World Cup 2026 host states and NCR only

grant_programs.name: Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant (Byrne-JAG)
grant_programs.typical_award_range: Formula-based by jurisdiction population and violent crime rate; small agencies may receive $5K–$50K; large cities $1M+
grant_programs.application_cycle: Annual formula grants. Solicitation typically released Q1. Direct awards to large jurisdictions; smaller agencies apply via state.
grant_programs.apply_via: bja.gov/byrnejag or through state JAG administrator
grant_programs.notes: DRONE Act (pending) would explicitly authorize Byrne-JAG for drone purchase/operation. Currently eligible if framed as crime prevention technology. Flexible use — fewer restrictions than HSGP.
grant_programs.ndaa_note: No explicit NDAA requirement under current rules, but agencies should verify with BJA

grant_programs.name: FEMA Assistance to Firefighters Grant (AFG) / SAFER
grant_programs.typical_award_range: AFG equipment grants: $5K–$2.5M depending on department size. 10% cost share required for local depts.
grant_programs.application_cycle: AFG: Annual; FY2025 application closed. FY2026 expected Q2-Q3 2026.
grant_programs.notes: Drones eligible under 'Equipment' category for fire suppression, SAR, and hazmat recon. Strong fit for fire-department-led DFR programs. Not law enforcement focused.

platforms.name: Skydio X10D

cops_tech.notes: Blue UAS listed — simplest procurement path

fema_bric.notes: NDAA compliant. Verify with FEMA program office.

recommended_documentation: Reference Blue UAS list in grant narrative
Skydio NDAA compliance letter (available from Skydio)

platforms.name: BRINC Lemur 2

cops_tech.notes: Blue UAS listed. Note: indoor tactical use case only.

platforms.use_case_note: Indoor tactical only — not a patrol DFR platform. Verify that indoor use case aligns with grant scope.

platforms.name: Flock Alpha

cops_tech.notes: NDAA compliant but NOT Blue UAS listed. Requires manufacturer NDAA certification letter. More documentation burden.

hsgp_shsp.notes: AEL eligible. Not Blue UAS — need Flock NDAA cert letter. Verify with SAA.

hsgp_uasi.notes: AEL eligible. Not Blue UAS — additional documentation required.

ohio_dfr_pilot.notes: Not on published ODOT pre-approved list (Skydio/BRINC/Parrot referenced). Verify with ODOT.

recommended_documentation: Flock Safety NDAA compliance certification letter
Confirm not on FCC Covered List (currently not listed)

platforms.name: Parrot ANAFI USA
platforms.foci_concern: moderate — French parent company. Verify FOCI disclosure requirements with SAA.

cops_tech.notes: Blue UAS listed. French parent — some agencies require FOCI disclosure. Verify with grant officer.

hsgp_shsp.notes: Blue UAS listed, AEL eligible. French parent Parrot — verify FOCI with SAA.

hsgp_uasi.notes: Blue UAS listed. FOCI disclosure may be required.

ohio_dfr_pilot.notes: Parrot explicitly referenced as approved vendor in Ohio RFA

fema_bric.notes: NDAA compliant. Used by FEMA — strong precedent.

platforms.foci_note: Parrot SA is a French company. US assembly does not eliminate foreign ownership consideration. Some grant programs and agency policies require FOCI disclosure. Always verify with SAA and legal counsel.
recommended_documentation: FOCI disclosure (if required by SAA or agency policy)

platforms.name: Inspired Flight IF800

ohio_dfr_pilot.notes: Not referenced in Ohio RFA documentation. Verify with ODOT.

platforms.production_note: Production capacity constrained vs. market demand as of early 2026. Verify lead times before committing in grant budget.

avoid_for_federal_grants: Any platform on FCC Covered List
fcci_flag_verify: Parrot ANAFI USA (French parent — verify with SAA)
quick_reference.blue_uas_note: Blue UAS listing is a procurement convenience tool, not a legal requirement for most grants. But it dramatically reduces documentation burden and SAA scrutiny.
quick_reference.key_rule: When in doubt: NDAA §848 compliant + not on FCC Covered List + ASDA compliant = eligible for most DFR grant programs. Confirm with SAA before purchase.
